task_completion.description: Open the hemoglobin beta multispecies FASTA file
task_completion.visual_evidence: The application window (e.g., UGENE) shows the loaded sequences from 'hemoglobin_beta_multispecies.fasta', displaying multiple species like Human, Mouse, Chicken, etc.

task_completion.description: Select the MUSCLE algorithm for multiple sequence alignment
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box, menu, or settings panel is visible showing 'MUSCLE' selected as the alignment algorithm.

task_completion.description: Execute the alignment and display the aligned sequences
task_completion.visual_evidence: The sequence viewer shows the completed alignment, characterized by gaps (dashes '-') inserted into the sequences to align conserved regions across the different species.
An 'Export' or 'Save As' dialog is open with the output format explicitly set to 'ClustalW' or '.aln'.

task_completion.description: Save the alignment to the specified results directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: The save dialog shows the exact file path '~/UGENE_Data/results/hemoglobin_alignment.aln', or a file manager/terminal verifies the file was created at this location.

integrity.description: The agent used the application GUI to perform the alignment rather than terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the bioinformatics software's graphical interface (menus, dialogs) rather than a terminal window running a 'muscle' command-line execution.

integrity.description: The alignment was genuinely computed by the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The alignment should appear in the software's sequence alignment viewer with proper coloring and consensus histograms, not just as raw text in a basic text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not copy-paste a pre-computed alignment.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no evidence of the agent opening a web browser to search for a pre-computed ClustalW alignment or pasting text directly into a text editor to bypass the MUSCLE tool.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input file contains exact UniProt hemoglobin beta subunit sequences for 8 species (accessions P68871, P02088, P02112, P02132, Q90485, P02070, P02062, P02067). The final output must be a ClustalW formatted alignment file saved exactly at ~/UGENE_Data/results/hemoglobin_alignment.aln.
